title: The Midnight Ink of Whitby Library
openingIncident: At 8:55 pm, Eleanor Ravenscroft, renowned antiquarian book dealer, was found dead in a cosy study lined with dark wood paneling, a battered armchair beside a brass lamp, a half‑filled mug on the desk and a well‑worn book stacked nearby. Three people had been present during the final minutes, and each gave a different account. Nothing outside the room is needed to identify the killer.

suspects.name: Gareth Pembroke
suspects.statement: At 8:45 pm, Victor Hale helped me move the chair clear of the doorway. We left the room together immediately afterwards. Clara Whitfield and Eleanor Ravenscroft were still inside.
suspects.alibiClaim: Victor Hale and I were together outside the room from 8:45 pm until the discovery at 8:55 pm.

suspects.name: Clara Whitfield
suspects.statement: I left at 8:40 pm. I never touched the lamp and did not go near the book.
suspects.alibiClaim: I had already left the room by 8:40 pm and did not return.

suspects.name: Victor Hale
suspects.statement: At 8:45 pm, while Gareth Pembroke moved the chair clear of the doorway, I returned the book squarely to its place. We then left together and remained together until the body was found at 8:55 pm.
suspects.alibiClaim: Gareth Pembroke and I were together outside the room from 8:45 pm until the discovery at 8:55 pm.

clues.title: The Dented Lamp
clues.detail: A fresh blood smear and a new impact dent mark the lamp. Eleanor Ravenscroft has a fresh blunt wound to the head.

clues.title: Movement of Chair
clues.detail: A fresh floor scrape made by the chair is crossed by a narrow blood droplet from the attack whose edge remains unbroken. Gareth Pembroke and Victor Hale both state that they moved the chair clear of the doorway at 8:45 pm before leaving together.

clues.title: Fibres on Lamp
clues.detail: Fine copper-coloured silk threads are caught inside a fresh split beneath the base of the lamp. Clara Whitfield's copper silk pocket square has a fresh narrow tear, exposing the same alternating copper and navy stripe.

clues.title: Position of Book
clues.detail: A clean rectangular outline left when the book was moved is crossed by a narrow blood droplet from the attack. Victor Hale states that they returned the book squarely to its place at 8:45 pm immediately before leaving the room.

clues.title: Crack in Mug
clues.detail: A crack runs across the mug. A continuous yellowed glue line is visible deep inside the break.

solution.motive: Clara Whitfield killed Eleanor Ravenscroft because the imminent sale of a priceless medieval manuscript that would have left her bankrupt.
solution.method: Eleanor Ravenscroft was killed by a heavy blow from the lamp.
solution.timeOfDeath: After 8:45 pm and before discovery at 8:55 pm
solution.opportunity: Clara Whitfield claimed to have left at 8:40 pm. The physical trace on the chair corroborates the action described by Gareth Pembroke, while the trace on the book corroborates the action described by Victor Hale. They left together at 8:45 pm, and both traces are crossed by evidence from the later attack. Meanwhile, fine copper-coloured silk threads on the lamp directly contradict Clara Whitfield's denial and place them at the murder object during the attack window.
